purpose: Assesses the probability that multiple consensus assumptions fail simultaneously, providing a structured way to evaluate tail risk of consensus unraveling
components: Enumeration of required consensus assumptions with individual failure probabilities
Identified 20-30% probability of 3+ consensus assumptions failing simultaneously
Structured table format for systematic tracking
current_assessment: Pair_3 called this 'the most analytically useful contribution' from the challenger. The 20-30% joint failure probability represents a non-trivial tail risk that the demographic consensus may unravel.
limitations: Assumption failure probabilities are themselves subjective estimates
Independence assumption in joint probability calculation may not hold (assumptions may be correlated)
Does not specify which combination of failures is most consequential
Framework requires continuous updating as new evidence arrives
